numerical_facts.context: Louis Jordan says that he set off on his 35-foot sailboat from South Carolina in late January.
His sailboat would capsize two more times before crew members on a German-flagged container ship, the Houston Express, spotted Jordan about 200 miles off the North Carolina coast on Thursday.

Son vindicated as father rescued after 12 days at sea.
After bad weather hit, Ron Ingraham told CNN affiliate KHNL/KGMB that his 25-foot sailboat went "backwards all night long."
The 67-year-old used a rope to pull himself back in.
A distress call went out, prompting a search that would cover 12,000 square miles.
Twelve days after that first distress call, Ron Ingraham was picked up about 64 miles (103 kilometers) south of Honolulu "weak, hungry and dehydrated" and -- most importantly -- alive.
